text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: Safari has a built-in "Translate this page" feature. When a user has it enabled (or triggers it manually) while the datepicker's calendar popup is open, Safari's translation pass walks the DOM and rewrites text nodes inside whatever is currently on screen -- including the open calendar dialog. That DOM rewrite fights with React's own reconciliation of the same nodes: users report the calendar breaking, freezing, or throwing errors on any interaction (clicking a day, changing month, etc.) once the page has been auto-translated by Safari while the calendar was open.

The calendar popup is exactly the kind of live, interactive, non-prose UI that should never be rewritten by a browser's page-translation pass -- unlike static article text, mutating it out from under React corrupts the widget. Browsers support an opt-out for this on a per-element basis; the calendar dialog element should use it so Safari (and other browsers with similar auto-translate features) leaves the calendar's DOM alone entirely.

Fix the library source so the rendered calendar dialog element opts out of browser auto-translation. Do not just add a workaround in a test file -- change the actual component that renders the calendar dialog.

Environment notes: package manager is Yarn (Berry, via corepack) -- already installed and `yarn install --immutable` already run for you. Existing tests can be run as a regression check with `NODE_ENV=test yarn test` (or `NODE_ENV=test node_modules/.bin/jest` to run the whole suite directly). This is a TypeScript + React codebase; the calendar dialog markup lives under `src/`.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll trace the calendar dialog component and its existing render tests, then make the smallest source change and run the focused regression checks plus relevant type/lint validation.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg -n \"calendar|dialog|translate|role=\\\"dialog\\\"|role: \\\"dialog\\\"\" src tests __tests__ 2>/dev/null | head -200 && rg --files src | head -100"

text: The dialog is centralized in `src/calendar_container.tsx`; its non-inline wrapper already owns the `role="dialog"` and modal attributes. I’ll add the standard HTML `translate="no"` opt-out to that wrapper, and cover both dialog and inline behavior so the source contract is explicit.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,120p' src/calendar.tsx && rg -n \"CalendarContainer|container=\" src/calendar.tsx src/test/calendar_container.test.tsx src/test/calendar_test.test.tsx | head -80 && git diff -- package.json"

text: I’m applying the opt-out directly to the wrapper component. Because the same wrapper renders inline calendars too, the attribute will be present on the calendar UI in both modes; this keeps the interactive calendar protected without changing any public props or custom-container behavior.
The source and regression assertion are in place. The focused Jest test, formatting check, and ESLint are still running; I’m waiting for their results before checking the final diff.

text: The focused suite passes all 8 tests, and both Prettier and ESLint pass. I’m doing one final TypeScript check and reviewing the diff to ensure the only functional change is the dialog’s translation opt-out; the pre-existing `package.json` formatting change remains untouched.

Fixed the Safari translation issue by adding `translate="no"` to the calendar container dialog in [calendar_container.tsx](/home/user/workspace/src/calendar_container.tsx:25).

Added a regression assertion. Verified with:

- Focused Jest suite: 8 tests passed
- ESLint passed
- Prettier passed
- TypeScript check passed
